etymology.description: 酉(酉) (yǒu, "alcoholic drink vessel") + 告(告) (gào) = 酉("alcoholic drink vessel") provides meaning related to fermentation -> 告 serves as the phonetic element -> final form of 酷
etymology.interpretation: The character 酷(酷) (kù, "cool") combines 酉(酉) (yǒu, "alcoholic drink vessel") indicating fermentation or strong flavors, with 告(告) (gào) suggesting the phonetic sound, forming a character that conveys the sense of something intense or severe, historically applied to harshness or intensity, now colloquially adapted to mean 'cool' or 'stylish.'

examples.chinese: 他的风格很酷(他的風格很酷)。
这辆车看起来非常酷(這輛車看起來非常酷)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè liàng chē kàn qǐlái fēicháng kù.

examples.chinese: 她的新发型很酷(她的新髮型很酷)，让她看起来更有气质(讓她看起來更有氣質)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xīn fàxíng hěn kù, ràng tā kàn qǐlái gèng yǒu qìzhí.
examples.english: Her new hairstyle is cool, making her look more elegant.
